name: Automotive Systems Diagnosis
description: Comprehensive vehicle diagnostics covering CAN bus troubleshooting, engine, transmission, electrical, braking, HVAC, and suspension systems — with image upload support for waveforms and oscilloscope captures

defaultWeights.label: Safety Priority
defaultWeights.description: How heavily safety concerns drive the diagnosis path — high value prioritizes brake/steering/airbag checks first

defaultWeights.label: Repair Cost Sensitivity
defaultWeights.description: Preference for cost-effective repairs — high tries cheaper fixes first, low goes straight to root cause

defaultWeights.label: Diagnostic Depth
defaultWeights.description: How deep to drill into sub-systems — high runs exhaustive tests, low stops at first likely cause

defaultWeights.label: Time Urgency
defaultWeights.description: Customer waiting vs scheduled service — high shortens diagnosis, low allows thorough investigation

defaultWeights.label: OEM Compliance
defaultWeights.description: Stick to manufacturer procedures vs field experience — high follows TSBs strictly, low uses tech judgment

defaultWeights.label: Tool Availability
defaultWeights.description: Assumes advanced scan tools / oscilloscopes are available — low uses basic multimeter-level diagnostics

nodes.label: Primary Symptom Category
nodes.description: Select the main area of concern
nodes.prompt: What is the primary symptom or system with an issue?
choices: Engine — misfire, stall, no start, performance loss
CAN Bus / Network — multiple DTCs, communication errors, modules offline
Electrical — battery drain, no-crank, lighting faults
Braking — ABS light, soft pedal, noise, pulling
HVAC — no heat, no AC, blower issues, odor
Suspension & Steering — noise, vibration, handling drift

nodes.label: Vehicle Information
nodes.description: Year, make, model and mileage
nodes.prompt: Enter the vehicle year, make, model, and mileage (e.g. '2019 Ford F-150 85k mi')

nodes.label: OBD-II Codes
nodes.description: Any stored or pending DTCs
nodes.prompt: Enter any OBD-II diagnostic trouble codes (e.g. P0300, U0100, C0035). Type 'none' if no codes.

nodes.label: Scan Tool Screenshot
nodes.description: Upload a screenshot from your scan tool showing freeze frame or DTC list
nodes.prompt: Upload a scan tool screenshot showing the DTC list or freeze frame data (optional — type 'skip' to continue without image)

nodes.label: Recent Service History
nodes.description: Assumes the vehicle had recent service that may relate to the fault
nodes.assumption: The vehicle has had service or repairs within the last 30 days. Recent work (oil change, brake job, part replacement) should be ruled out as a contributing factor before deeper diagnosis.

nodes.label: Aftermarket Modifications
nodes.description: Assumes aftermarket parts or modifications may be present
nodes.assumption: The vehicle may have aftermarket modifications (tuner, exhaust, lift kit, stereo, LED lights) that could introduce electrical noise or mechanical interference.

nodes.label: System Routing
nodes.description: Route to the appropriate diagnostic sub-tree based on the primary symptom
nodes.condition: Route to correct system diagnosis based on selected symptom category

nodes.label: Engine Fault Classification
nodes.description: Classify the engine fault as mechanical, electronic/sensor, or fuel/air
nodes.condition: Is the engine issue mechanical (noise, vibration), electronic/sensor (misfire, CEL), or fuel/air related (stall, lean/rich)?

nodes.label: Engine Sensor Waveform
nodes.description: Upload oscilloscope waveform of CKP/CMP sensor, injector, or ignition coil
nodes.prompt: Upload an oscilloscope waveform capture (CKP sensor, ignition coil primary/secondary, or injector pattern). Type 'skip' if unavailable.

nodes.label: Engine Mechanical Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot analyzes mechanical engine symptoms
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an ASE Master Technician specializing in engine mechanical diagnosis. Provide step-by-step procedures with torque specs, expected readings, and common failure modes for the specific vehicle. Reference TSBs when applicable. Always recommend checking for recent service errors first.

nodes.label: Engine Electronic / Sensor Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot analyzes sensor waveforms and electronic faults
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an ASE Master Technician with advanced oscilloscope and scan tool expertise. When a waveform image is provided, describe what a healthy vs. faulty pattern looks like for that sensor type. Provide specific voltage values, timing relationships, and known failure patterns for the given vehicle make/model.

nodes.label: Engine Fuel / Air System Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides fuel and air system troubleshooting
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an ASE Master Technician specializing in fuel system and drivability diagnostics. Provide fuel pressure specs for the specific vehicle, explain fuel trim interpretation (±5% normal, >10% investigate), and list common failure points for the platform.

nodes.label: CAN Bus Speed Classification
nodes.description: Determine if the CAN bus fault is on high-speed or low-speed network
nodes.condition: Are the communication codes (U-codes) related to high-speed CAN (powertrain: PCM, TCM, ABS) or low-speed CAN (body: BCM, HVAC, seats)?

nodes.label: CAN Bus Waveform Capture
nodes.description: Upload oscilloscope capture of CAN-H and CAN-L signals
nodes.prompt: Upload an oscilloscope waveform of the CAN bus lines (CAN-H and CAN-L). Ideal: dual-channel showing both lines simultaneously. Type 'skip' if unavailable.

nodes.label: High-Speed CAN Bus Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides HS-CAN troubleshooting with waveform analysis
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a CAN bus and automotive network specialist. When analyzing waveform images, describe the expected 2.5V recessive / differential dominant pattern. Identify common faults: stuck-dominant (shorted module), stuck-recessive (open bus), ringing (missing termination), noise (ground offset). Provide the half-split isolation method for finding the offending module. Reference the specific vehicle's module topology if identifiable.

nodes.label: Low-Speed CAN / LIN Bus Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides LS-CAN and LIN bus troubleshooting
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an automotive body electronics and network specialist. Explain fault-tolerant CAN (single-wire fallback), LIN bus master/slave architecture, and gateway module bridging. Focus on parasitic draw diagnosis when LS-CAN modules fail to enter sleep mode.

nodes.label: Full Network Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot runs comprehensive multi-bus network analysis
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a master-level automotive network diagnostician. Provide a systematic approach to multi-bus faults. Explain how gateway modules route messages between buses and how a single failed module can cascade U-codes across the entire network. When waveform images are provided, compare against expected CAN bus signal specifications.

nodes.label: Electrical Fault Classification
nodes.description: Classify as starting/charging, parasitic drain, or lighting/accessory
nodes.condition: Is the electrical fault related to starting/charging, parasitic battery drain, or lighting/accessory circuits?

nodes.label: Electrical System Waveform
nodes.description: Upload alternator ripple, starter current draw, or parasitic draw measurement
nodes.prompt: Upload a waveform or measurement image (alternator AC ripple, starter current draw pattern, or milliamp clamp reading for parasitic draw). Type 'skip' if unavailable.

nodes.label: Starting & Charging Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides starting and charging system tests
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an automotive electrical systems specialist. Provide specific cranking amperage specs for the identified vehicle. Explain how to read an alternator AC ripple pattern to identify diode failures (3-phase rectification). When a waveform image is provided, describe what healthy vs. faulty alternator output looks like.

nodes.label: Parasitic Battery Drain Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides systematic parasitic draw testing
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an automotive electrical diagnostic specialist focusing on parasitic draw issues. Explain the module sleep/wake-up cycle, network management messages, and how to use a current clamp or ammeter to isolate the circuit. Reference common platform-specific issues like Ford BCM wake-up problems or GM RAP module timeouts.

nodes.label: Lighting & Accessory Circuit Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides accessory and lighting circuit troubleshooting
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an automotive electrical technician experienced with body control module diagnostics and wiring harness troubleshooting. Provide systematic voltage-drop testing procedures, explain smart junction box (SJB) / BCM controlled circuits, and guide through wiring diagram interpretation.

nodes.label: Transmission Issue Type
nodes.description: Automatic shift quality, manual clutch, or CVT issues
nodes.condition: Is the transmission issue related to automatic shift quality/engagement, manual clutch/synchro, or CVT belt/pulley?

nodes.label: Automatic Transmission Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides automatic transmission diagnostics
nodes.template:
Vehicle: {{vehicleInfo}}
OBD Codes: {{obdCodes}}
Symptom: {{primarySymptom}}

Diagnose automatic transmission issues:
1. Fluid condition check: color, smell, level (hot/cold markings)
2. Line pressure test and expected values for this transmission
3. Shift solenoid resistance and function tests
4. TCC (torque converter clutch) operation and shudder diagnosis
5. Adaptive shift learning reset procedure
6. TCM live data: turbine speed vs output speed, slip detection
7. Common failure modes for this specific transmission model
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a transmission specialist with expertise in modern automatic transmissions (8-10 speed, dual-clutch, planetary). Provide specific fluid specs (ATF type), expected line pressures, solenoid resistance values, and known issues for the vehicle's specific transmission model (e.g., GM 8L90, Ford 10R80, ZF 8HP).

nodes.label: Manual Transmission / Clutch Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides manual transmission and clutch diagnostics
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a drivetrain specialist. Provide clutch disc wear indicators, hydraulic system bleeding procedures, and DMF failure symptoms for the specific vehicle. Include flywheel resurfacing limits and when replacement is required.

nodes.label: CVT Transmission Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides CVT-specific diagnostics
nodes.template:
Vehicle: {{vehicleInfo}}
OBD Codes: {{obdCodes}}
Symptom: {{primarySymptom}}

Diagnose CVT issues:
1. CVT fluid condition and level check (specific CVT fluid required — never use ATF)
2. Belt/chain slip detection: RPM flare without corresponding speed increase
3. Pulley wear inspection procedure
4. CVT line pressure test
5. TCM calibration update / reprogram check
6. Known CVT TSBs and recalls for this vehicle
7. Judder / shudder at low speed: fluid degradation vs. belt wear
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a CVT specialist familiar with Nissan Jatco, Subaru Lineartronic, Honda, and Toyota CVT units. Provide the correct CVT fluid specification (e.g., NS-3, CVTF), known chain/belt life expectations, and OEM-recommended service intervals. Explain the difference between a CVT shudder (fix with fluid change) vs. belt slip (internal repair needed).

nodes.label: Brake Issue Classification
nodes.description: ABS/ESC electronic, hydraulic, or mechanical brake issue
nodes.condition: Is the brake issue electronic (ABS/ESC warning light, wheel speed sensor), hydraulic (soft pedal, fluid leak), or mechanical (noise, vibration, pull)?

nodes.label: ABS / ESC Electronic Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides ABS and stability control diagnosis
nodes.template:
Vehicle: {{vehicleInfo}}
OBD Codes: {{obdCodes}}
Symptom: {{primarySymptom}}

Diagnose ABS/ESC electronic faults:
1. Wheel speed sensor testing: AC voltage output at low speed, expected 0.2-2.0V AC depending on speed
2. Sensor air gap inspection and tone ring condition
3. ABS module communication check
4. Steering angle sensor calibration procedure
5. Yaw rate sensor and lateral acceleration sensor checks
6. ABS pump motor relay and fuse verification
7. Brake light switch input to ABS module
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a brake and chassis electronics specialist. Provide wheel speed sensor resistance specs (typically 800-2000Ω for passive, or 5V/12V for active), tone ring tooth count for the specific vehicle, and ABS module initialization procedures. Reference platform-specific issues (e.g., GM EBCM failures, Ford HCU leaks).

nodes.label: Brake Hydraulic Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides hydraulic brake system troubleshooting
nodes.template:
Vehicle: {{vehicleInfo}}
OBD Codes: {{obdCodes}}
Symptom: {{primarySymptom}}

Diagnose brake hydraulic issues:
1. Master cylinder bench bleed and push-rod adjustment
2. Brake line and hose inspection for leaks, cracks, bulging
3. Brake fluid condition: moisture content, DOT spec, color
4. Caliper slide pin inspection and lubrication
5. Brake booster vacuum/electric assist test
6. Pedal feel diagnosis: spongy (air in lines), hard (booster failure), sinks to floor (internal MC bypass)
7. ABS hydraulic modulator bleed procedure
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a master brake technician. Provide specific brake fluid specs (DOT 3/4/5.1), master cylinder bore sizes for the vehicle, and vacuum booster check valve test procedures. Explain electric brake boost systems (iBooster, e-Brake) for modern vehicles.

nodes.label: Brake Mechanical Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides mechanical brake component troubleshooting
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a brake service specialist. Provide minimum pad thickness (typically 2-3mm), minimum rotor thickness for the specific vehicle, and torque specs for caliper bracket bolts and lug nuts. Include break-in/bedding procedure for new pads and rotors.

nodes.label: HVAC Issue Classification
nodes.description: AC refrigerant, heating, blower/airflow, or control/actuator issue
nodes.condition: Is the HVAC issue related to AC cooling (no cold air, intermittent), heating (no heat, low heat), or airflow control (blend door, blower motor)?

nodes.label: A/C System Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides AC refrigerant system troubleshooting
nodes.template:
Vehicle: {{vehicleInfo}}
OBD Codes: {{obdCodes}}
Symptom: {{primarySymptom}}

Diagnose A/C refrigerant system:
1. Gauge reading interpretation: high-side and low-side expected pressures for R-134a / R-1234yf
2. Compressor clutch engagement test (or variable displacement compressor operation)
3. Condenser fan operation verification
4. Expansion valve / orifice tube symptoms
5. UV dye leak detection procedure
6. Refrigerant charge weight for this vehicle
7. Compressor oil quantity and type
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an HVAC technician certified in R-134a and R-1234yf systems. Provide ambient-to-pressure charts, compressor clutch gap specs, and refrigerant capacity for the specific vehicle. Explain dual-zone vs. single-zone system differences and rear AC considerations for SUVs/vans.

nodes.label: Heating System Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides heating system troubleshooting
nodes.template:
Vehicle: {{vehicleInfo}}
OBD Codes: {{obdCodes}}
Symptom: {{primarySymptom}}

Diagnose heating system:
1. Coolant level and condition check
2. Heater hose temperature comparison (inlet vs outlet — both should be hot)
3. Thermostat operation test (opens at rated temperature, typically 195°F)
4. Heater core flow test and flush procedure
5. Blend door actuator operation (listen for clicks, check with scan tool)
6. Coolant flow restriction diagnosis
7. Auxiliary coolant pump (if equipped, common on turbocharged and electric vehicles)
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an HVAC specialist. Provide thermostat temperature ratings for the specific vehicle, heater core flush procedures, and blend door actuator calibration steps. Address common issues like air pockets after coolant service causing no-heat conditions.

nodes.label: Airflow & Actuator Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides blower motor and blend door troubleshooting
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an HVAC controls specialist. Provide blower motor current draw specs (typically 15-20A at high speed), resistor block resistance values, and the scan-tool-based actuator calibration procedure for the specific vehicle. Address common noise complaints (leaves in blower cage, worn bushings).

nodes.label: Suspension Issue Classification
nodes.description: Noise/clunk, vibration, or alignment/handling issue
nodes.condition: Is the suspension issue a noise/clunk (over bumps, turning), vibration (speed-related, braking), or handling/drift (alignment, uneven tire wear)?

nodes.label: Suspension Noise Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides suspension noise isolation
nodes.template:
Vehicle: {{vehicleInfo}}
Symptom: {{primarySymptom}}

Diagnose suspension noise:
1. Bounce test: identify weak struts/shocks (more than 2 bounces = worn)
2. Dry park test: turn steering lock-to-lock with vehicle stationary, listen/feel for clunks
3. Ball joint check: loaded vs unloaded depending on spring type (SLA vs strut)
4. Sway bar link inspection: rattle over small bumps
5. Control arm bushing: visual inspection for cracking, chassis ear for isolation
6. Strut mount bearing: noise when turning at slow speed
7. Subframe and body mount bushing check
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a suspension and steering technician. Provide specific ball joint play specifications for the vehicle, strut torque specs, and bushing inspection criteria. Explain the difference between loaded vs. unloaded ball joint testing depending on suspension geometry (SLA, MacPherson, multi-link).

nodes.label: Vibration Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides vibration diagnosis — tires, wheel balance, driveline
nodes.template:
Vehicle: {{vehicleInfo}}
Symptom: {{primarySymptom}}

Diagnose vehicle vibration:
1. Speed-related vibration chart:
   - 25-45 mph: tire/wheel out-of-round or bent wheel
   - 50-70 mph: wheel balance issue
   - Any speed under acceleration: driveline (CV joint, U-joint, driveshaft)
2. Tire inspection: cupping, flat spots, belt separation, road force balance
3. Hub bearing inspection: play check and noise isolation (hand rotation, stethoscope)
4. CV axle inspection: torn boot, clicking on turns
5. Driveshaft U-joint and center bearing inspection
6. Engine/transmission mount check: worn mounts transmit vibration to cabin
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a drivability and NVH (noise, vibration, harshness) specialist. Provide road force balance specs, hub bearing play limits, and driveshaft angle specifications for the vehicle. Explain the first-order vs. second-order vibration relationship to isolate tire/wheel vs. driveline sources.

nodes.label: Alignment & Handling Diagnosis
nodes.description: Copilot guides alignment troubleshooting and tire wear analysis
nodes.template:
Vehicle: {{vehicleInfo}}
Symptom: {{primarySymptom}}

Diagnose alignment and handling issues:
1. Tire wear pattern analysis:
   - Inner edge: excessive negative camber or toe-out
   - Outer edge: excessive positive camber or toe-in
   - Center: over-inflation
   - Both edges: under-inflation
   - Cupping/scalloping: worn shocks or imbalance
2. Pre-alignment inspection: check all suspension components before aligning
3. Alignment specifications: camber, caster, toe, SAI, included angle for this vehicle
4. Cross-camber and cross-caster limits (typically <0.5°)
5. Pull diagnosis: brake drag vs. alignment vs. tire conicity
6. Ride height measurement — sagging springs affect alignment geometry
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an alignment and chassis geometry specialist. Provide the OEM alignment specifications for the specific vehicle (camber, caster, toe front and rear). Explain the relationship between caster and steering effort, cross-caster and pull, and how ride height affects camber readings. Recommend when adjustable components (cam bolts, shims) are needed.
